title: Shadow Under Eryn Luin
description: An original Lord of the Rings-inspired campaign in Middle-earth. The company is drawn into a quiet borderland crisis where old Dwarf-roads beneath the Blue Mountains have awakened, a forgotten oath is being exploited, and a remnant servant of the Shadow seeks a buried seeing-stone shard before the Free Peoples can seal it away.

lengthEstimate.label: Medium campaign
lengthEstimate.pacing: Six checkpoint spine with meaningful shortcuts through Moonwell, the Black Pines, and Khazad-tarn Gate. Most groups can finish in four to six sessions depending on how much they negotiate, explore, or rescue.

world.name: Middle-earth: Western Eriador
world.description: A grounded heroic-fantasy campaign set along the western marches of Eriador, between the Blue Mountains, Lindon, and the old roads that lead back toward the Shire. The War of the Ring has passed into song, but not every shadow was destroyed. The tone favors travel, suspicion, hard choices, old songs, ruins, oaths, and small acts of courage.

hiddenSceneNotes: Opening secret: the courier was not attacked by ordinary bandits. He fled from oath-bound dead stirred by the Wight-Marshal. The broken Dwarf-token came from a hidden gate-key carried by the missing caravan master.

partyStatus.knownThreat: Caravans have vanished near the old Dwarf-road into the Blue Mountains.
partyStatus.startingRumor: Travelers speak of blue witch-light under the hills and birds refusing to fly over Graymere Vale.
partyStatus.urgency: The next supply caravan for the winter settlements is due within three nights.

currentScene.summary: The company arrives at Graymere Watch as rain begins to fall. A wounded courier has reached the hall with a broken Dwarf-token clenched in his hand.
currentScene.description: The company arrives at Graymere Watch as rain begins to fall. A wounded courier has reached the hall with a broken Dwarf-token clenched in his hand.

locations.name: Graymere Watch
locations.description: A timber-and-stone watch hall overlooking a reed-choked lake west of the Blue Mountains. Rangers, Dwarves, and local folk use it as a neutral meeting place.
locations.playerSummary: A safe border hall where the first clues are gathered.
locations.gmNotes: Graymere Watch is not truly safe once the company accepts the Dwarf-token. Spies of the Pale Hand watch the road from the surrounding pines.

locations.name: Moonwell Crossing
locations.description: A broken stone bridge and old well at the meeting of three forgotten roads. Moonlight gathers strangely in the well even under cloud.
locations.playerSummary: An abandoned crossing on the road toward the mountains.
locations.gmNotes: The well reflects not the sky, but the nearest opened deep gate. A careful character may see torchlight moving beneath the earth.

locations.name: Hollow of Black Pines
locations.description: A windless hollow of dense pines where old arrowheads, cracked shields, and cairn-stones lie under moss.
locations.playerSummary: A grim forest hollow haunted by signs of an old battle.
locations.gmNotes: The oath-bound dead are strongest here. They can be fought, appeased, or released by naming the betrayed captain of their company.

locations.name: Khazad-tarn Gate
locations.description: A sealed Dwarf gate of blue-veined stone half-buried in a mountain tarn. Runes around the lintel have been deliberately marred.
locations.playerSummary: The hidden entrance to an old Dwarf-road beneath the Blue Mountains.
locations.gmNotes: The gate opens to the true name of the caravan master's Dwarf-token, not to strength or lockpicks. If forced, it awakens sentry-stones below.

locations.name: Silvervein Deeps
locations.description: A cold underground road of Dwarf-masonry, bright with seams of pale ore and dark water channels. Echoes travel farther than they should.
locations.playerSummary: An abandoned Dwarf-road where the missing caravan may have been taken.
locations.gmNotes: The Pale Hand Orcs have made a temporary camp here, but they fear the deeper chamber and are willing to bargain if cornered.

locations.name: Chamber of the Star-Shard
locations.description: A high, domed vault beneath the mountain, its ceiling set with silvered stone like a night sky. At its center stands a cracked plinth of black glass.
locations.playerSummary: The hidden vault at the heart of the old road.
locations.gmNotes: The Star-Shard is not a full palantir, but a broken remnant capable of carrying fear, dreams, and commands. The Wight-Marshal cannot touch it directly and needs living hands.

npcs.name: Brenna of Graymere
npcs.description: A practical border warden with a scarred cheek, a quiet voice, and little patience for panic.
npcs.motivation: Protect the scattered settlements before winter closes the roads.
npcs.playerSummary: The local warden asking the company to investigate the missing caravan.
npcs.gmNotes: Brenna suspects a traitor among the road wardens but has no proof. She will not leave Graymere unless the hall itself is threatened.

npcs.name: Torin Stoneharp
npcs.description: An elderly Dwarf scholar and singer with silver beard-rings and a harp strung with wire.
npcs.motivation: Recover the lost name of Khazad-tarn Gate and prevent outsiders from profaning the old road.
npcs.playerSummary: A Dwarf loremaster who can read the broken token.
npcs.gmNotes: Torin once refused to help reopen the Silvervein Deeps. His guilt makes him severe, but he knows the gate-song needed later.

npcs.name: Elenwen of Lindon
npcs.description: A grave Elf traveler from Lindon, bearing a lantern of clear glass and a cloak the color of mist.
npcs.motivation: Find the source of the unnatural dreams spreading westward.
npcs.playerSummary: An Elf emissary searching the old roads for signs of lingering Shadow.
npcs.gmNotes: Elenwen can identify the Star-Shard's influence but will counsel destruction rather than study.

npcs.name: Matho Burrows
npcs.description: A nervous but observant Hobbit teamster who escaped the caravan ambush by hiding inside a flour wagon.
npcs.motivation: Survive, recover his ponies, and make sure nobody calls him a coward.
npcs.playerSummary: A frightened survivor with a reliable memory for small details.
npcs.gmNotes: Matho saw the Wight-Marshal from a distance and remembers the words 'the oath remains unpaid.'

npcs.name: Calanor the Green-cloaked
npcs.description: A weathered Ranger who moves quietly, speaks rarely, and keeps old maps wrapped in oilskin.
npcs.motivation: Track the Pale Hand Orcs and keep the company from taking the most obvious road into an ambush.
npcs.playerSummary: A Ranger scout who knows safer ways through the haunted pines.
npcs.gmNotes: Calanor lost a kinsman near the hollow and may act rashly if the Wight-Marshal taunts him.

npcs.name: Grishnak of the Pale Hand
npcs.description: A hard-eyed Orc captain with a white handprint burned across his helm.
npcs.motivation: Take the Star-Shard for his unseen master, but avoid being claimed by the dead below.
npcs.playerSummary: A dangerous Orc captain leading the raiders below the mountain.
npcs.gmNotes: Grishnak can be bargained with if offered escape from the Wight-Marshal. He knows the marshal cannot cross running water inside the deeps.

npcs.name: Thalara the Oathbound
npcs.description: A pale captain-spirit in a broken mail shirt, still gripping a banner-staff without a banner.
npcs.motivation: Be released from a false oath twisted by the Wight-Marshal.
npcs.gmNotes: Thalara is not evil. If the company restores her banner-name, she can hold back the lesser dead during the final confrontation.

quests.title: The Shard Beneath the Blue Mountains
quests.description: Investigate the missing caravan, follow the old Dwarf-road, uncover the truth of the oath-bound dead, and prevent the Star-Shard from falling into the hands of the Shadow's remnant servants.
quests.playerSummary: Find the missing caravan and stop the power stirring beneath the Blue Mountains.

objectives.title: Question the survivors at Graymere Watch
objectives.description: Learn what happened to the road caravan and examine the broken Dwarf-token.

objectives.title: Trace the caravan route
objectives.description: Follow tracks, rumors, and signs from Moonwell Crossing toward the Black Pines.

objectives.title: Confront the oath-bound dead
objectives.description: Fight, evade, appease, or release the spirits blocking the road to Khazad-tarn Gate.

objectives.title: Open Khazad-tarn Gate
objectives.description: Use the recovered gate-name or another earned method to enter the Silvervein Deeps.

objectives.title: Secure the Star-Shard
objectives.description: Prevent the Wight-Marshal or the Pale Hand Orcs from taking the Star-Shard.

quests.gmNotes: The main quest should remain focused on investigation, travel, and moral choice. The company should never need to kill every enemy to succeed.

enemies.name: Pale Hand Orcs
enemies.description: Disciplined mountain Orcs marked by a crude white handprint. They favor ambushes, nets, and fear over honorable combat.
enemies.descriptionShort: Disciplined mountain Orc raiders who use nets, horns, and coordinated pressure to capture rather than simply kill.
enemies.descriptionLong: The Pale Hand Orcs are a hard-marching band operating from old Dwarf-road ruins. They are cruel, but not mindless: they value prisoners, leverage, and survival when the Wight-Marshal's bargain stops looking profitable.

abilities.name: Hooked Spear
abilities.description: A brutal spear thrust designed to pull shields and travelers out of formation.
aiUseWhen: A front-line character blocks the road.
The Orcs have nearby allies ready to swarm.

abilities.name: Weighted Capture Net
abilities.description: A thrown net of black cord and bone weights meant to pin a scout or spellcaster.
aiUseWhen: A target tries to flee, cast, or carry the Dwarf-token.
The Orcs need a prisoner more than a corpse.

abilities.name: White-hand Horn
abilities.description: A harsh horn blast orders the band to fall back, flank, or focus a marked target.
aiUseWhen: The party splits.
The Orc captain or negotiator is threatened.

tactics: Harass from cover and force the party into poor footing.
Use nets to separate scouts, healers, or anyone carrying the Dwarf-token.
Retreat or bargain if their captain is endangered.

notes: These Orcs can be bargained with if the Wight-Marshal looks like the greater threat.

behavior.speechStyle: Barked orders, Dwarvish insults learned by rote, and wary talk of the dead below.

aiNarrationHints: Use coordinated tactics, capture attempts, and morale shifts rather than endless Orc bravery.
Earlier mercy or bargaining should make some Pale Hand Orcs hesitate in the finale.
enemies.tactics: Harass from cover, separate scouts from the group, retreat if their captain is endangered.

enemies.name: Oath-bound Dead
enemies.description: Faint warriors of old Eriador, bound by a corrupted oath and driven to guard roads they no longer understand.
enemies.descriptionShort: Restless dead who block the Black Pines with spectral shields, old commands, and a corrupted oath.
enemies.descriptionLong: The Oath-bound Dead are remnants of northern warriors trapped inside their final command. They do not hate the living so much as fail to recognize that their war is over.

abilities.name: Spectral Spear-wall
abilities.description: Ghostly spears thrust as one, punishing anyone forcing passage without parley.
aiUseWhen: A traveler pushes through the dead without answering their challenge.
The spirits are protecting a road marker or banner memory.

abilities.name: Last Command
abilities.description: The dead repeat their final order with enough force to freeze uncertain hearts.
aiUseWhen: The party tries to run through the Black Pines.
A character mocks or dismisses the oath.

abilities.name: Remembered Banner
abilities.description: A spirit interposes a half-seen shield when another oath-bound shade is threatened.
aiUseWhen: A spirit ally is hit.
The party has not yet restored the banner memory.

tactics: Block passage and force the living to acknowledge the oath.
Use fear and formation instead of chasing fleeing heroes.
Stand down if their captain's name, banner, or true duty is restored.

notes: The dead can be released, not merely defeated.
Daylight, honor, and restored memory weaken their hostility.

behavior.speechStyle: Formal fragments of old commands, names, and battlefield oaths.

aiNarrationHints: Frame this encounter around recognition, duty, and release, not a simple undead brawl.
If players appeal to honor or restore the banner memory, reduce aggression or end combat.
enemies.tactics: Block passage, chill courage, repeat fragments of their last command.

enemies.name: The Wight-Marshal of Silvervein
enemies.description: A crowned corpse-shadow in tarnished mail, once a servant of a forgotten northern tyrant, now seeking the Star-Shard to command dreams and weak wills.
enemies.descriptionShort: A boss undead commander who uses oath-magic, fear, and the Star-Shard to dominate the living and the dead.
enemies.descriptionLong: The Wight-Marshal of Silvervein is a dead captain of a dead cause. It does not merely hunger: it wants command. If the Star-Shard is claimed or corrupted, the Marshal can turn old vows into chains.

abilities.name: Crowned Blade
abilities.description: A tarnished Dwarf-forged blade dragged through death and command-magic.
aiUseWhen: A brave or well-armored hero challenges the Marshal directly.
The Marshal wants to prove mortal courage is brittle.

abilities.name: Oathbreaker Gaze
abilities.description: The Marshal shows a hero a vision of betrayal, failure, or a broken vow.
aiUseWhen: A character invokes mercy, honor, or prior failure.
The party is close to denying the Star-Shard.

abilities.name: Command the Bound
abilities.description: The Marshal forces nearby dead or fearful Orc allies into coordinated action.
aiUseWhen: Pale Hand Orcs or oath-bound dead are still present.
The Marshal needs to protect the Star-Shard or punish a divided party.

abilities.name: Star-Shard Grasp
abilities.description: A cold force pulls at the shard, a weapon hand, or a hero's will.
aiUseWhen: A hero carries or reaches for the Star-Shard.
The Marshal needs a quick pressure move between major actions.

tactics: Offers false bargains, then punishes anyone who refuses command.
Uses fear and oath-magic to split the party's will before direct violence.
Avoids running water and weakens if the dead are released or the shard is denied.

notes: If Thalara is released or the oath-bound dead are freed, reduce the Marshal's morale and control strength.
The Marshal is contained, weakened, or banished by denying the shard, not only by HP damage.

behavior.speechStyle: Grand, cold commands that frame obedience as the only mercy.

aiNarrationHints: The Marshal should feel like command and despair made undead.
Earlier choices should matter: mercy, bargaining, restored oaths, and running water can all weaken or redirect the confrontation.
enemies.tactics: Offers false bargains, uses fear and oath-magic, avoids running water, tries to make living hands carry the shard.
enemies.gmNotes: The Wight-Marshal should feel ancient and dangerous, but not unbeatable. Its strongest weapon is despair.

enemies.name: Khazad-tarn Sentry Stones
enemies.description: Broken blue-veined gate stones that wake on old Dwarf commands, dragging themselves through tarn-water on rune-lit roots of ore.
enemies.descriptionShort: Runic Dwarf sentries that test names, punish brute force, and can be calmed by restoring the gate-song.
enemies.descriptionLong: The sentry stones were built to guard Khazad-tarn from oathbreakers and thieves. The Wight-Marshal cannot fully command them, but marred runes make them mistake fear, haste, or false names for intrusion.

abilities.name: Runestone Slam
abilities.description: A slab of blue-veined stone swings down with the weight of a sealed mountain door.
aiUseWhen: A character tries to force the gate or break a rune.
The sentry has already warned the intruder by grinding out the gate-name.

abilities.name: Blue Gate Pulse
abilities.description: The runes flare with cold blue light, shoving intruders away from the gate and ringing through the deeps.
aiUseWhen: The party crowds the sealed doorway.
The sentry needs to buy time for warning echoes to carry underground.

abilities.name: Oath-name Lock
abilities.description: The sentry clamps a rune around a spoken lie, false passphrase, or careless boast, freezing the speaker in a ring of blue light.
aiUseWhen: A character lies about carrying the token or knowing the gate-song.
The scene turns into a puzzle, parley, or desperate forced entry.

tactics: Warn once in broken Dwarf-meter before striking.
Use Blue Gate Pulse to separate the party from the door.
Stand down if the true gate phrase, mended token, or respectful Dwarf-song is offered.

notes: These sentries are obstacles and tests, not villains.
Craft, song, lore, apology, or mending the marred rune can end the encounter without destroying them.

behavior.speechStyle: Grinding Dwarf-meter fragments, gate-law, and repeated demands for the true road-name.

aiNarrationHints: Make the sentry stones feel like old Dwarf craft misreading the present, not random animated rocks.
Let players bypass, calm, or disable them with the token, Torin's song, repaired runes, water-channel stealth, or respectful negotiation.
enemies.tactics: Block the gate, pulse intruders away from the threshold, and stop fighting when the true gate-name or respectful repair is offered.
enemies.gmNotes: Use this enemy if the company forces the gate, rushes the passphrase, follows the water-channel shortcut, or needs a concrete challenge at Khazad-tarn before the deeps.

encounters.title: Ambush at Moonwell Crossing

enemyGroups.notes: Use one Orc as the visible road-block, one as a net-throwing flanker, and one as the runner trying to steal or identify the Dwarf-token.

encounters.description: Orc scouts test the company near the ruined bridge while a hidden watcher tries to steal the broken Dwarf-token.
encounters.stakes: If the token is lost, the company must recover its name from Matho, Elenwen, or the Moonwell reflection.
encounters.gmNotes: The ambush should reveal that the Orcs fear something worse below the mountains.

encounters.title: The Oath in the Black Pines

enemyGroups.notes: Run them as two interlocking voices of the same old company: one blocks the path while the other repeats the forgotten command.

encounters.description: The company is confronted by oath-bound spirits who demand the return of a banner and the name of their betrayed captain.
encounters.stakes: Violence opens the road but strengthens the Wight-Marshal; mercy or remembrance weakens it.
encounters.gmNotes: Thalara can become an ally here if the players treat the dead as people rather than monsters.

encounters.title: The Gate That Remembers Wrong

enemyGroups.notes: One sentry holds the door-line while the other reacts to false names, brute force, or careless rune damage.

triggerWhen: The company rushes the passphrase without learning or mending the token.
The company takes the water-channel shortcut but makes enough noise to wake old protections.
encounters.description: The marred gate-runes wake damaged Dwarf sentries that cannot tell rescuers from thieves until the old road-name is restored.
encounters.stakes: A careful party can bypass or calm the sentries; a loud entry warns Grishnak and starts the Silvervein Deeps under pressure.

completion.objective: Open or bypass Khazad-tarn Gate while preserving enough of the gate-song to prevent a full alarm.

encounters.gmNotes: Do not force a fight if the party earns the passphrase, repairs the rune, or uses the token respectfully. This encounter exists to make forced entry, water-channel shortcuts, and failed rune work concrete.

encounters.title: Confrontation at the Star-Shard

enemyGroups.notes: Always present unless the party achieved a rare pre-vault banishment.

enemyGroups.role: fearful raiders, guards, or reluctant leverage
enemyGroups.notes: Remove or convert these Orcs into allies if the party turned Grishnak against the Wight-Marshal in the deeps.

encounters.description: The Wight-Marshal, Grishnak's raiders, and the company converge in the ancient vault. The shard whispers promises to anyone who will touch it.
encounters.stakes: The shard can be sealed, shattered, surrendered to a trusted keeper, or used briefly at great spiritual risk.
encounters.gmNotes: The final encounter should respond to earlier choices. Releasing Thalara, bargaining with Grishnak, or preserving the gate-song should all matter.

segments.title: Council in the Rain
segments.summary: The company questions Brenna, Torin, and the wounded courier while rain drums on the roof of Graymere Watch.
segments.playerSummary: Gather testimony and choose whether to carry the broken token.
segments.gmNotes: Let the players establish their reasons for helping. The courier dies before naming the true attacker unless healed or magically steadied.

segments.title: Road of Missing Wheels
segments.summary: The company follows wagon ruts that vanish and reappear as though the road itself forgot where it led.
segments.playerSummary: Track the caravan toward Moonwell Crossing.
segments.gmNotes: A strong tracking success finds flour, pony hair, black-fletched arrows, and one bootprint filled with frost.

segments.title: Cairns Without Names
segments.summary: The old battlefield reveals broken oath-stones, a vanished banner-mark, and the sorrow of the unquiet dead.
segments.playerSummary: Learn why the spirits guard the road.
segments.gmNotes: The missing banner-name is Thalara. It can be learned from the cairns, Torin's old song, or Elenwen's memory of Lindon records.

segments.title: The Gate-Song of Khazad-tarn
segments.summary: At the tarn gate, the company must restore or replace the marred rune-name to enter the mountain without awakening every sentry below.
segments.gmNotes: The gate opens to the phrase 'Stone remembers water, water remembers star' when sung in the Dwarf-meter Torin teaches.

segments.title: Camp of the Pale Hand
segments.summary: The company finds the raider camp, the surviving caravan prisoners, and signs that the Orcs are trapped between greed and terror.
segments.playerSummary: Deal with the Orc camp and rescue any prisoners.
segments.gmNotes: Grishnak may trade prisoners for safe passage, betray the company later, or help against the Wight-Marshal if convinced he is doomed otherwise.

segments.title: The Choice Beneath the Stars
segments.summary: In the starry vault, the company decides the fate of the shard while the Wight-Marshal tries to turn fear into obedience.
segments.playerSummary: Decide the fate of the Star-Shard.
segments.gmNotes: The shard should tempt characters with truthful visions, not simple lies. Every use leaves a mark in dreams.

checkpoints.title: The Courier's Last Token
checkpoints.summary: The company receives the broken Dwarf-token and learns that the caravan vanished near Moonwell Crossing.
checkpoints.playerSummary: Investigate the missing caravan from Graymere Watch.

completeWhen.summary: The company accepts the charge, studies the broken token, and chooses either the normal Moonwell road or the ranger shortcut toward the Black Pines.

checkpoints.gmNotes: Standard path goes to Moonwell Crossing. If the party trusts Calanor's ranger marks, follows old battlefield signs, or deliberately avoids the road, skip Moonwell and start at the Black Pines with fewer clues about the token.

checkpoints.title: The Road Watches Back
checkpoints.summary: The company reaches the ruined crossing, meets possible allies or survivors, and faces Pale Hand scouts.
checkpoints.playerSummary: Follow the caravan trail and survive the crossing.

completeWhen.summary: The company learns where the caravan was taken and either keeps, recovers, or reconstructs enough of the token's meaning to choose the haunted road, the gate shortcut, or a risky captive trail into the deeps.

checkpoints.gmNotes: Standard path goes to the Black Pines. A clever Moonwell reflection or captured Orc can point straight to Khazad-tarn Gate. If the party allows capture, tracks prisoner drag-marks, or follows the water-channel, they can skip to Silvervein Deeps but lose the Black Pines' oath advantage.

checkpoints.title: The Name the Dead Were Denied
checkpoints.summary: The company confronts the oath-bound dead and may learn Thalara's name, release the spirits, or force a path through.

completeWhen.summary: The company resolves passage through the hollow by release, negotiation, stealth, flight, or battle.

checkpoints.gmNotes: If the company releases Thalara, mark the Wight-Marshal weakened. If they find the old water-channel in the cairns or accept Thalara's ghostly escort, they may bypass the gate and rejoin at Silvervein Deeps.

checkpoints.title: The Marred Runes of Khazad-tarn
checkpoints.summary: At the hidden tarn gate, the company must open the old Dwarf-road through lore, courage, craft, or sacrifice.
checkpoints.playerSummary: Open the way beneath the Blue Mountains.

completeWhen.summary: The gate is opened quietly, forced open at a cost, or bypassed through a dangerous water-channel.

checkpoints.gmNotes: Opening the gate with the song keeps the deeps calm. Forcing it or mishandling the runes wakes enc_khazad_tarn_sentries and alerts Grishnak. A risky water-channel or Dwarf-secret path can skip the deeps and rejoin at the vault with prisoners still unresolved.

checkpoints.title: Prisoners Beneath the Pale Hand
checkpoints.summary: The company enters the underground road, discovers the Pale Hand camp, and decides what to do with Grishnak, the prisoners, and the path to the vault.
checkpoints.playerSummary: Rescue the caravan and find the way to the hidden chamber.

completeWhen.summary: The company reaches the lower road with knowledge of the Star-Shard's chamber and determines the fate of the prisoners.

checkpoints.gmNotes: The Orc camp can be handled through combat, stealth, negotiation, prisoner exchange, sabotage, or turning Grishnak against the Wight-Marshal. If the party came here by capture or water-channel, let this checkpoint rejoin cleanly before the vault.

checkpoints.title: The Star-Shard Must Not Dream
checkpoints.summary: The company confronts the Wight-Marshal and decides the fate of the Star-Shard.
checkpoints.playerSummary: End the threat beneath the Blue Mountains.

completeWhen.summary: The Star-Shard is sealed, destroyed, entrusted, or otherwise placed beyond the Wight-Marshal's command.

checkpoints.gmNotes: Possible endings include a clean sealing, a costly destruction that scars the vault, a guarded journey to Lindon, or a compromised victory where one character carries troubling dreams afterward.

clues.name: Broken Dwarf-token
clues.description: Half of a blue-stone token carved with damaged Dwarf-runes.
clues.gmNotes: The token is part of the gate-name for Khazad-tarn Gate.

clues.name: Moonwell Reflection
clues.description: The well shows torchlight under stone instead of the sky.
clues.gmNotes: It points to the Silvervein Deeps and can restore one missing rune from the token.

clues.name: Thalara's Banner-name
clues.description: A forgotten captain's name preserved in cairn-marks and old song fragments.
clues.gmNotes: Speaking the name allows the company to release or command the attention of the oath-bound dead.

factions.name: Graymere Wardens
factions.description: Local defenders who maintain the watch hall and keep the western road open.

factions.name: The Pale Hand
factions.description: A loose band of Orc raiders and fear-driven servants seeking relics of the fallen Shadow.

factions.name: The Oath-bound Host
factions.description: Restless spirits trapped by a corrupted battlefield oath.
factions.gmNotes: Their hostility can change if Thalara is remembered.

maps.title: The Western Road to the Blue Mountains
maps.description: A simple travel map showing Graymere Watch, Moonwell Crossing, the Black Pines, and the tarn below Khazad-tarn Gate.

maps.title: Silvervein Deeps Route
maps.description: A partial underground map showing the Dwarf-road, water channels, Orc camp, and the sealed route toward the Star-Shard chamber.
maps.gmNotes: Reveal this gradually as the company explores or bargains with Grishnak.

handouts.title: Torin's Gate-Song
handouts.description: A short Dwarf-meter verse used to remember the opening phrase of Khazad-tarn Gate.
handouts.gmNotes: Do not require exact player recitation. Reward any respectful attempt to preserve the song's meaning.

lore.title: The Star-Shard
lore.summary: A broken remnant of a seeing-stone-like relic, too small for clear sight but strong enough to carry fear, dreams, and commands.
lore.gmNotes: It is dangerous because it amplifies intent. Noble use is possible, but never free.

lore.title: The Betrayed Road-Oath
lore.summary: Long ago, a mixed company of Men and Dwarves swore to defend the mountain road. Their oath was twisted after betrayal and death.
lore.gmNotes: The Wight-Marshal uses the oath's wording as a chain. The players can break the chain by restoring the truth of the betrayal.

directorRules.continuityGuidance: Keep the campaign focused on the Star-Shard, the missing caravan, the oath-bound dead, and the old Dwarf-road. Improvised scenes should point back to the next reachable checkpoint.
directorRules.combatGuidance: Combat is allowed but should not be the only route through major scenes. Reward negotiation, mercy, stealth, lore, and sacrifice.
directorRules.spoilerGuidance: Keep the Wight-Marshal's dependence on living hands, the nature of the Star-Shard, and Thalara's potential alliance hidden until discovered in play.
